name: Radhanagar Beach
description: Awarded Asia's Best Beach, Radhanagar is a paradise of turquoise waters, white sand, and lush greenery. The stunning sunset views are unmatched anywhere in India.
longDescription: Radhanagar Beach on Havelock Island is consistently ranked among the best beaches in Asia. Its crystal-clear turquoise waters, powdery white sand, and dense tropical forest backdrop create a surreal setting. The beach stretches over 2 km and offers a gentle slope into the sea, making it perfect for swimming. Sunsets here are legendary — the sky transforms into shades of gold, orange, and crimson as the sun dips below the horizon.

name: Palolem Beach
description: A crescent-shaped paradise in South Goa with calm waters, colorful beach huts, and a laid-back vibe perfect for relaxation and dolphin spotting.
longDescription: Palolem Beach is a stunning crescent-shaped bay lined with swaying palm trees in South Goa. Known for its calm, shallow waters and vibrant beach shack culture, it's perfect for swimming, kayaking, and dolphin spotting. Silent noise parties and the magical bioluminescence during monsoon season make it truly special.

name: Varkala Beach
description: Dramatic red laterite cliffs overlooking the Arabian Sea with natural springs, cliff-top cafés, and spiritual significance at the Papanasam shore.
longDescription: Varkala is unique among Indian beaches for its dramatic setting atop towering red laterite cliffs that offer spectacular views of the Arabian Sea. The cliff-top walkway is lined with bohemian cafés, Ayurvedic spas, and craft shops. The Papanasam Beach below is considered sacred — Hindus believe that bathing here washes away sins. Natural springs add to its mystical charm.

name: Agonda Beach
description: One of Goa's most serene and unspoiled beaches, known for Olive Ridley turtle nesting sites. A perfect escape from the tourist crowds.
longDescription: Agonda Beach is a hidden gem in South Goa that remains remarkably unspoiled despite its beauty. The 3-km stretch of golden sand is backed by gentle hills covered in coconut palms. It's an important nesting site for Olive Ridley sea turtles, and conservation efforts are active during the nesting season. The calm waters and peaceful ambiance make it a favorite among those seeking solitude.

name: Marina Beach
description: India's longest urban beach stretching 13 km along the Bay of Bengal. A cultural landmark of Chennai with iconic lighthouses and heritage buildings.
longDescription: Marina Beach in Chennai is the longest natural urban beach in India and one of the longest in the world, stretching over 13 kilometers. While not ideal for swimming due to strong undercurrents, it's a cultural icon — flanked by numerous statues, heritage buildings, and the iconic Chennai lighthouse. The beach comes alive in the evenings with street food vendors and local families.
highlights: Longest urban beach in India (13 km)

name: Tarkarli Beach
description: Maharashtra's best-kept secret with crystal-clear water visibility up to 20 feet. Famous for scuba diving and water sports at the Sindhudurg coast.
longDescription: Tarkarli is a breathtaking beach destination in Sindhudurg district of Maharashtra. What sets it apart is the remarkably clear water — visibility extends up to 20 feet, making it one of the best spots for snorkeling and scuba diving in mainland India. The confluence of the Karli River and the Arabian Sea creates a unique ecosystem. The nearby Sindhudurg Fort adds historical significance.

name: Kovalam Beach
description: Three crescent beaches separated by rocky headlands, crowned by the iconic Vizhinjam Lighthouse. Kerala's most famous beach destination.
longDescription: Kovalam consists of three crescent-shaped beaches separated by rocky headlands. The Lighthouse Beach is the most popular, offering excellent swimming conditions with lifeguard patrols. The iconic red-and-white Vizhinjam Lighthouse provides panoramic views of the coast. The area is also famous for its Ayurvedic resorts and traditional Kerala cuisine.

name: Calangute Beach
description: The Queen of Beaches — Goa's largest and most popular beach with vibrant nightlife, water sports, and a buzzing shack culture.
longDescription: Known as the 'Queen of Beaches,' Calangute is the largest beach in North Goa and one of the most visited in India. The golden sand stretches for miles and is lined with beach shacks, restaurants, and shops. It's a hub for water sports including parasailing, jet skiing, and banana boat rides. The nightlife scene here is legendary with beach parties and live music.

name: Om Beach
description: Named for its OM-shaped coastline, this spiritual beach in Gokarna blends natural beauty with a backpacker's paradise.
longDescription: Om Beach in Gokarna gets its name from its unique shape that resembles the sacred Hindu symbol 'OM' when viewed from above. Two semi-crescent shapes merge to form this distinctive coastline. The beach offers a perfect blend of spirituality, adventure, and natural beauty. Trekking between the beaches of Gokarna is a popular activity, and the beach is accessible by both road and a scenic coastal trek.

name: Elephant Beach
description: A snorkeler's paradise with vibrant coral reefs and marine life. One of the most beautiful beaches in the Andaman archipelago.
longDescription: Elephant Beach is a pristine stretch of white sand accessible by a 30-minute trek through dense tropical forest or by boat from Havelock Island's main jetty. The beach is famous for its spectacular coral reefs visible even in shallow waters. Snorkeling here reveals a kaleidoscope of colorful corals, tropical fish, and occasionally sea turtles. Glass-bottom boat rides are available for non-swimmers.

name: Baga Beach
description: Goa's party capital where the Baga Creek meets the sea. Famous for Tito's Lane, Saturday Night Market, and thrilling water sports.
longDescription: Baga Beach is the ultimate party destination in Goa. The Baga Creek at the northern end creates a scenic setting, and the famous Tito's Lane is known for its pulsating nightlife. During the day, paragliding, jet skiing, and banana boat rides keep the adrenaline flowing. The Saturday Night Market at Arpora is a must-visit for its eclectic mix of shopping and live entertainment.

name: Chandipur Beach
description: The 'Hide and Seek' beach where the sea recedes up to 5 km during low tide, creating a unique landscape you can walk on.
longDescription: Chandipur is one of the most unique beaches in the world. During low tide, the sea recedes by approximately 5 kilometers, creating a vast, walkable expanse of wet sand. This 'vanishing sea' phenomenon allows visitors to literally walk on the seafloor, discovering shells, crabs, and marine creatures left behind. The red crabs that cover the beach during certain times create a spectacular visual.
highlights: Sea recedes 5 km during low tide

name: Marari Beach
description: A secluded fishing village beach near Alleppey backwaters — voted among the top 'hammock beaches' in the world by National Geographic.
longDescription: Marari Beach is a pristine, secluded beach in the fishing village of Mararikulam near Alleppey. National Geographic once listed it among the world's top 'hammock beaches.' The beach remains wonderfully uncommercialized, with traditional fishing boats (kettuvallam) dotting the shore. Its proximity to the famous Alleppey backwaters makes it a unique combo destination — beach by day, houseboat by evening.

name: Gokarna Main Beach
description: A beautiful town beach flanked by the ancient Mahabaleshwar Temple and lush green hills. The perfect blend of spirituality and scenic beauty.
longDescription: Gokarna Main Beach is a beautiful stretch of sand that borders the ancient temple town of Gokarna. Unlike the more secluded beaches nearby, the main beach is easily accessible and offers views of the hills and the sea. The nearby Mahabaleshwar Temple is one of the most sacred Shiva temples in South India. The town itself has a laid-back, spiritual vibe that attracts pilgrims and beach lovers alike.

name: Anjuna Beach
description: The birthplace of Goa's legendary trance scene. Known for its flea market, rocky shores, and bohemian counter-culture heritage.
longDescription: Anjuna Beach is steeped in Goa's hippie history and is widely regarded as the birthplace of the Goa trance music scene. The beach features dramatic rocky outcrops and a mix of sandy stretches. The Wednesday Flea Market is a Goa institution, selling everything from handicrafts to vintage clothing. The beach retains its bohemian, free-spirited atmosphere that first attracted travelers in the 1960s.

name: Kaup Beach
description: A serene black-sand beach crowned by a 100-year-old lighthouse. One of Karnataka's hidden coastal treasures near Udupi.
longDescription: Kaup Beach (also called Kapu Beach) is a scenic coastal gem near Udupi in Karnataka. The beach is famous for its striking 100-year-old lighthouse perched on a rocky peninsula. Visitors can climb the lighthouse for panoramic views of the Arabian Sea. The beach features unique black volcanic sand interspersed with golden stretches, and the rocky tide pools are fascinating to explore.

name: Paradise Beach
description: Accessible only by boat through the Chunnambar Backwaters, this secluded golden beach lives up to its name as a true paradise.
longDescription: Paradise Beach in Puducherry is a secluded, pristine stretch of golden sand accessible only by a scenic boat ride through the Chunnambar Backwaters. This exclusivity keeps it uncrowded and naturally beautiful. The beach has clean, fine golden sand and relatively calm waters suitable for swimming. The boat journey through mangrove-lined backwaters adds to the experience, making the destination feel truly like paradise.

name: Kudle Beach
description: A beautiful crescent-shaped beach in Gokarna surrounded by lush green hills. Popular for camping under the stars and beach bonfires.
longDescription: Kudle Beach is a gorgeous crescent-shaped beach nestled between lush green hills in Gokarna. It's wider and longer than nearby Om Beach and has a more relaxed, hippie vibe. The beach is popular for camping, with many travelers setting up tents on the sand and enjoying bonfires under the stars. Beach shacks offer fresh seafood and refreshing drinks while hammocks sway between palm trees.

name: Mandarmani Beach
description: The longest driveable beach in India where you can ride your car on the hard-packed sand along the Bay of Bengal.
longDescription: Mandarmani is unique as one of the few beaches in India where you can drive your vehicle directly on the beach. The 13-km stretch of hard-packed sand along the Bay of Bengal makes for a thrilling driving experience. The beach is relatively uncrowded compared to other popular destinations and is known for the red crabs that dot the shoreline. Resorts line the beach road, offering comfortable stays with sea views.
highlights: Drive on the beach for 13 km

name: Bangaram Beach
description: An uninhabited coral island with crystal-clear lagoon waters and pristine white sand. India's answer to the Maldives.
longDescription: Bangaram Island in Lakshadweep is an uninhabited teardrop-shaped coral island surrounded by a stunning turquoise lagoon. Often called 'India's Maldives,' the island offers unmatched privacy and pristine beauty. The coral reefs around the island are home to exotic marine life including manta rays, sea turtles, and colorful reef fish. With only one resort on the island, it offers an exclusive, once-in-a-lifetime experience.

name: Butterfly Beach
description: Goa's most secluded beach accessible only by boat or a challenging jungle trek. A hidden paradise where butterflies dance among the trees.
longDescription: Butterfly Beach is one of Goa's best-kept secrets, accessible only by boat from Palolem or via a challenging trek through the jungle. Named after the colorful butterflies that flit among the trees, especially during sunrise and early morning hours. The beach is small but incredibly beautiful, with golden sand flanked by rocky cliffs covered in dense vegetation. Dolphins are frequently spotted in the waters off the beach.
highlights: Accessible only by boat or trek

name: Rushikonda Beach
description: Known as the Jewel of the East Coast, this golden beach is a hub for surfing and water sports near the beautiful city of Vizag.
longDescription: Rushikonda Beach, often called the 'Jewel of the East Coast,' is located on the outskirts of Visakhapatnam. The beach is flanked by lush green hills and offers a beautiful setting for swimming and water sports. It has become one of South India's emerging surfing destinations with several surf schools offering lessons. The Andhra Pradesh Tourism Department maintains the beach well with proper facilities.

name: Neil Island Beach
description: Home to the natural rock formation 'Howrah Bridge' and stunning coral reefs. A quieter alternative to Havelock with equal beauty.
longDescription: Neil Island (now officially Shaheed Dweep) is a serene island in the Andaman archipelago known for its natural rock formation called 'Howrah Bridge' — a coral arch formed by years of wave erosion. The island has several beautiful beaches including Laxmanpur (famous for sunsets), Bharatpur (best for snorkeling), and Sitapur (sunrise spot). The island moves at a wonderfully slow pace with bicycle being the primary mode of transport.

name: Dhanushkodi Beach
description: A ghost town beach at the tip of Rameswaram where the Bay of Bengal meets the Indian Ocean. Steeped in mythology and haunting beauty.
longDescription: Dhanushkodi sits at the south-eastern tip of Pamban Island in Rameswaram, where the waters of the Bay of Bengal and Indian Ocean merge. This ghost town was destroyed by a cyclone in 1964 and was never rebuilt. The ruins of the old church and railway station add an eerie beauty. According to Hindu mythology, this is where Lord Rama built a bridge to Lanka. The converging seas with their different colors create a mesmerizing visual.

name: Muzhappilangad Beach
description: Asia's longest drive-in beach stretching 4 km along the Malabar Coast. Perfect for a scenic beach drive with waves alongside.
longDescription: Muzhappilangad Drive-In Beach near Kannur is the longest drive-in beach in Asia, stretching approximately 4 km along the northern Kerala coast. The firm, flat sand allows vehicles to drive right along the waterline. Black rocks jutting out of the sea add drama to the landscape. The beach is also popular for paragliding, with the hills above offering perfect launch points and the beach serving as the landing zone.
highlights: Longest drive-in beach in Asia (4 km)
